questions.question: Should formatTimestampLabel and formatBucketRangeLabel tests pin a specific timezone (e.g. via TZ env var) to avoid locale-dependent failures in CI vs local dev?
questions.why_it_matters: These functions use toLocaleDateString/toLocaleTimeString which produce different output depending on the runtime's timezone and locale. Tests will be flaky without a deterministic approach.

questions.question: No vitest.config.ts exists — the vite.config.ts has the @ path alias but no test block. Should the test file import via relative paths or should we add a test config with resolve.alias?
questions.why_it_matters: The existing tests use @/ imports (e.g. format.test.ts imports from @/lib/utils/format). If vitest resolves these through vite.config.ts automatically, we can do the same; otherwise the new test will fail to resolve imports.

questions.question: formatSignedCurrency delegates to formatCurrency from @/lib/utils/format. Should we mock formatCurrency to isolate the unit, or test the composed behavior directly (matching existing test style)?
questions.why_it_matters: The existing format.test.ts tests formatCurrency directly without mocks. Using the same approach for formatSignedCurrency means tests double-cover formatCurrency logic, but matches the repo convention of no mocks for pure functions.

questions.question: getOutcomeColor uses nullish coalescing (?? '#818cf8') after index-modulo lookup. With noUncheckedIndexedAccess enabled in tsconfig, the return type includes undefined — should tests specifically verify the fallback path (negative indices, NaN)?
questions.why_it_matters: The ?? fallback only activates if OUTCOME_COLORS[index % length] returns undefined, which in practice only happens with NaN % length = NaN. Testing this edge case validates the defensive coding.

assumptions.assumption: Vitest resolves the @/ path alias from vite.config.ts automatically (since existing tests like format.test.ts already use @/ imports successfully)
assumptions.if_wrong: Need to add a vitest.config.ts with resolve.alias or use relative imports in the test file

assumptions.assumption: Tests should follow the existing convention: vitest imports (describe/it/expect), @/ path aliases, concise one-liner assertions where possible, describe blocks per function
assumptions.if_wrong: Would need to restructure tests to match a different convention

assumptions.assumption: For locale-dependent functions (formatTimestampLabel, formatBucketRangeLabel), we can test structural properties (contains expected substrings, correct format pattern) rather than exact string matches to avoid timezone/locale flakiness
assumptions.if_wrong: May need to set TZ=UTC and mock locale, or use regex-based assertions

assumptions.assumption: No mocking is needed — all six functions are pure (or locale-dependent but deterministic for a given environment) and have no external dependencies beyond formatCurrency which is itself pure
assumptions.if_wrong: Would need vi.mock() for formatCurrency in formatSignedCurrency tests

assumptions.assumption: The test file should be placed at frontend/src/__tests__/chartUtils.test.ts matching the existing flat test directory structure
assumptions.if_wrong: Task description explicitly specifies this path

assumptions.assumption: OUTCOME_COLORS array has 6 entries, so index wrapping tests should verify indices 0-5 return distinct colors and index 6 wraps to index 0's color
assumptions.if_wrong: Would need to adjust test expectations if array size differs
